title: विष्णु ध्यानम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु ध्यानम् ॥

शान्ताकारं भुजगशयनं पद्मनाभं सुरेशं
विश्वाधारं गगनसदृशं मेघवर्णं शुभाङ्गम्।
लक्ष्मीकान्तं कमलनयनं योगिभिर्ध्यानगम्यं
वन्दे विष्णुं भवभयहरं सर्वलोकैकनाथम्॥ १॥

कायेन वाचा मनसेन्द्रियैर्वा
बुद्ध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतेः स्वभावात्।
करोमि यद्यत्सकलं परस्मै
नारायणायेति समर्पयामि॥ २॥

ध्यायेत्सदा सवितृमण्डलमध्यवर्ती
नारायणः सरसिजासनसन्निविष्टः।
केयूरवान् मकरकुण्डलवान् किरीटी
हारी हिरण्मयवपुर्धृतशङ्खचक्रः॥ ३॥

मेघश्यामं पीतकौशेयवासं
श्रीवत्साङ्कं कौस्तुभोद्भासिताङ्गम्।
पुण्योपेतं पुण्डरीकायताक्षं
विष्णुं वन्दे सर्वलोकैकनाथम्॥ ४॥

सशङ्खचक्रं सकिरीटकुण्डलं
सपीतवस्त्रं सरसीरुहेक्षणम्।
सहारवक्षःस्थलकौस्तुभश्रियं
नमामि विष्णुं शिरसा चतुर्भुजम्॥ ५॥

नमो ब्रह्मण्यदेवाय गोब्राह्मणहिताय च।
जगद्धिताय कृष्णाय गोविन्दाय नमो नमः॥ ६॥

मङ्गलं भगवान् विष्णुर्मङ्गलं गरुडध्वजः।
मङ्गलं पुण्डरीकाक्षो मङ्गलायतनो हरिः॥ ७॥

ऊँ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय।
ऊँ नमो नारायणाय।
ऊँ नमो विष्णवे महाविष्णवे॥ ८॥

त्वमेव माता च पिता त्वमेव
त्वमेव बन्धुश्च सखा त्वमेव।
त्वमेव विद्या द्रविणं त्वमेव
त्वमेव सर्वं मम देवदेव॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु ध्यानम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Dhyanam ||

Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam Padmanabham Suresham
Vishvadharam Gaganasadrisham Meghavarnam Shubhangam |
Lakshmikantam Kamalanayanam Yogibhir Dhyanagamyam
Vande Vishnum Bhavabhayaharam Sarvalokaikanatham || 1 ||

Kayena Vacha Manasendriyairva
Buddhyatmana Va Prakriteh Svabhavat |
Karomi Yadyat Sakalam Parasmai
Narayanayeti Samarpayami || 2 ||

Dhyayet Sada Savitri Mandala Madhyavarti
Narayanah Sarasijasana Sannivishtah |
Keyuravan Makara Kundalavan Kiriti
Hari Hiranmayavapur Dhrita Shankha Chakrah || 3 ||

Meghashyamam Pitakausheyavasam
Shrivatsankam Kaustubhodbhasitangam |
Punyopetam Pundarikaayataksham
Vishnum Vande Sarvalokaikanatham || 4 ||

Sashankha Chakram Sakirita Kundalam
Sapita Vastram Sarasiruhekshanam |
Sahara Vakshah Sthala Kaustubha Shriyam
Namami Vishnum Shirasa Chaturbhujam || 5 ||

Namo Brahmanya Devaya Go Brahmana Hitaya Cha |
Jagaddhitaya Krishnaya Govindaya Namo Namah || 6 ||

Mangalam Bhagavan Vishnur Mangalam Garudadhvajah |
Mangalam Pundarikaksho Mangalayatano Harih || 7 ||

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya |
Om Namo Narayanaya |
Om Namo Vishnave Mahavishnave || 8 ||

Tvameva Mata Cha Pita Tvameva
Tvameva Bandhushcha Sakha Tvameva |
Tvameva Vidya Dravinam Tvameva
Tvameva Sarvam Mama Devadeva || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Dhyanam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शान्त स्वरूप, शेषनाग की शय्या पर शयन करने वाले, कमलनाभ, सुरेश, विश्व के आधार, आकाश सदृश, मेघवर्ण, शुभांग, लक्ष्मीकान्त, कमलनयन, योगियों द्वारा ध्यान से प्राप्य, भवभय हरने वाले, सर्वलोकनाथ विष्णु को वन्दन।

शरीर, वाणी, मन, इन्द्रियों, बुद्धि, आत्मा अथवा प्रकृति स्वभाव से जो कुछ भी करता हूँ, वह सब परम नारायण को समर्पित करता हूँ।

सदा सूर्यमण्डल के मध्य में विराजमान, कमलासन पर बैठे, केयूर, मकरकुण्डल, किरीट, हार, हिरण्मय शरीर, शंख-चक्रधारी नारायण का ध्यान करें।

मेघश्याम, पीतवस्त्रधारी, श्रीवत्सांकित, कौस्तुभ से भासित, पुण्यशील, पुण्डरीकाक्ष (कमलनयन), सर्वलोकनाथ विष्णु को वन्दन।

आप ही माता हैं, पिता हैं, बन्धु हैं, सखा हैं, विद्या हैं, धन हैं — हे देवदेव, आप ही मेरा सब कुछ हैं।
benefits: Collection of the most important Vishnu meditation shlokas
Includes the iconic Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam dhyana shloka
Contains complete surrender prayer 'Tvameva Mata Cha Pita'
Ideal for daily morning meditation and Vishnu puja
Invokes peace, protection and spiritual realization
description: The Vishnu Dhyanam, known in Sanskrit as विष्णु ध्यानम्, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Vishnu. Rooted in Vishnu Purana / Padma Purana, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant peace, protection and spiritual realization as well as include the iconic Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam dhyana shloka. Additionally, it is known to collection of the most important Vishnu meditation shlokas. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Vaikuntha Ekadashi and Devuthani Ekadashi for enhanced blessings. At 9 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 5 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Dhyanam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Dhyanam meditation shlokas in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Includes Shantakaram and Tvameva Mata for daily Vishnu worship.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vishnu Dhyanam and which verses does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Dhyanam (विष्णु ध्यानम्) is a sacred collection of nine of the most important Sanskrit dhyana (meditation) shlokas to Lord Vishnu, drawn from the Vishnu Purana, Padma Purana, and the broader Vaishnava stotra tradition. The nine shlokas comprise the most beloved and universally recited Vishnu meditation verses in Hinduism: (1) the iconic Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam Padmanabham Suresham — the dhyana-shloka of Vishnu reclining on Shesha-naga; (2) Kayena Vacha Manasendriyair-va — the supreme surrender mantra dedicating all actions to Narayana; (3) Dhyayet Sada Savitri-mandala-madhyavarti — Narayana in the centre of the sun-mandala; (4) Megha-shyamam Pitakausheyavasam — Vishnu's iconographic form; (5) Sashankha-chakram Sakirita-kundalam — the four-armed conch-discus form; (6) Namo Brahmanya-devaya — the prayer to Krishna-Govinda; (7) Mangalam Bhagavan Vishnu — the Mangala benediction; (8) Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya — the supreme Vasudeva mula-mantra; (9) Tvameva Mata Cha Pita Tvameva — the total surrender prayer. (Sources: Vishnu Purana; Padma Purana; classical Vaishnava prayer tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam?
seoFaqs.answer: Shantakaram Bhujagashayanam Padmanabham Suresham, Vishvadharam Gaganasadrisham Meghavarnam Shubhangam — Lakshmikantam Kamalanayanam Yogibhir Dhyanagamyam, Vande Vishnum Bhavabhayaharam Sarvalokaikanatham — is one of the most beloved verses in all Vaishnava devotion. It means: I salute Lord Vishnu, of the peaceful form (shanta-akara), who reclines upon the serpent Shesha (bhujaga-shayana), from whose navel sprouts the lotus (padma-nabha), the lord of the gods (sura-isha), the support of the universe (vishva-adhara), vast like the sky (gagana-sadrisha), of the colour of dark clouds (megha-varna), with auspicious limbs (shubha-anga), the beloved of Lakshmi (Lakshmi-kanta), with eyes like lotus petals (kamala-nayana), attainable only by the meditation of yogis (yogibhir dhyana-gamya), the destroyer of the fear of samsara (bhava-bhaya-hara), the one Lord of all the worlds (sarva-loka-eka-natha). It is the standard opening dhyana for any Vishnu puja. (Sources: Vishnu Sahasranama purvanga; classical Vaishnava puja paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Tvameva Mata Cha Pita verse?
seoFaqs.answer: Tvameva Mata Cha Pita Tvameva, Tvameva Bandhushcha Sakha Tvameva, Tvameva Vidya Dravinam Tvameva, Tvameva Sarvam Mama Devadeva — You alone are my mother and father, You alone are my brother and friend, You alone are my knowledge and wealth, You alone are my everything, O God of gods. This verse is one of the most universally chanted prayers in Hinduism, recited at the conclusion of nearly every puja, parayanam, and devotional gathering. It is the supreme expression of bhakti-yoga: total identification of the devotee's entire web of relationships and resources with the Divine, leaving nothing outside the divine relationship. The verse is from the Vishnu Stuti corpus (some attribute it to the Markandeya Purana) and is recited in Vishnu, Krishna, Rama, Vitthal, Venkateshwara, and all Vaishnava temples worldwide. It represents the culmination of sharanagati (total surrender). (Sources: classical Vishnu Stuti corpus; standard Hindu puja-paddhati conclusion.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Vishnu Dhyanam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Dhyanam is most powerful on Thursday (Guruvar — sacred to Vishnu and the Guru) and Saturday (Shanivar — Vishnu as Hari, the remover of sins). The most auspicious days of the year are: every Ekadashi (especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi in Margashirsha — the supreme Vishnu day), Devuthani Ekadashi (Kartik Shukla Ekadashi — Vishnu's awakening from yoga-nidra), Shayani Ekadashi (Ashadha Shukla Ekadashi — Vishnu's sleeping), Chaturmasya (the four-month vrat of Vishnu's yoga-nidra), Krishna Janmashtami, Ram Navami, Narasimha Jayanti, Vamana Jayanti, Akshaya Tritiya, and the four months of Chaturmasya. Daily morning meditation immediately after bath — facing east, before the home shrine, with closed eyes visualising Vishnu — is the prescribed sadhana. The Tvameva Mata verse is also recited at the end of every puja. (Sources: Drikpanchang Ekadashi calendar; traditional Vishnu upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Vishnu Dhyanam different from Vishnu Sahasranama Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are Vishnu meditation prayers but differ in length, source and ritual function. The Vishnu Dhyanam (this composition) is a curated collection of nine of the most beloved standalone Vishnu dhyana-shlokas — including Shantakaram, Tvameva Mata, Mangalam Bhagavan, Vasudeva mula-mantra — drawn from various sources (Vishnu Purana, Padma Purana, Markandeya Purana). It is suitable for general Vishnu meditation, daily morning prayer, and conclusion of any puja. The Vishnu Sahasranama Dhyanam is the specific six-verse opening section of the Vishnu Sahasranama (Mahabharata Anushasana Parva 149) — beginning with Shuklambaradharam Vishnum and Vyasam Vasishtha-naptaram — recited only as the dhyana-segment immediately before the 1000-name parayanam itself. The Vishnu Dhyanam is for general Vishnu meditation; the Sahasranama Dhyanam is the ritual entry-point into the Sahasranama parayanam. (Sources: Mahabharata Anushasana Parva 149; classical Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily recitation of Vishnu Dhyanam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Dhyanam grants comprehensive benefits because it combines the most powerful Vishnu prayers in a single recitation: (1) bhava-bhaya-hara — destruction of the fear of samsara through the Shantakaram dhyana, (2) total surrender (sharanagati) through the Kayena Vacha and Tvameva Mata verses — placing the entire life under Vishnu's care, (3) protection through the Shankha-chakra-gada-padma-bearing four-armed Vishnu visualisation, (4) shanti — peace through meditation on the peaceful (shanta-akara) form, (5) prosperity through Lakshmi-kanta Vishnu's grace, (6) auspiciousness through the Mangalam Bhagavan Vishnu benediction, (7) sin-destruction through the Vasudeva mula-mantra Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya (Bhagavata Purana's bija-mantra), (8) Vaikuntha-prapti — the supreme abode of Vishnu, (9) spiritual realization (yogibhir dhyana-gamyam) through systematic meditation on Vishnu's nine dhyana-rupas. It is one of the most complete daily Vishnu sadhanas. (Sources: Vishnu Purana; Padma Purana; classical Vaishnava devotional tradition.)
